safest_plan.entry_basis: Newer 2025-06-02 grade: '18-19 (spot or low leverage)'. Matches the HTF demand block drawn on both charts (19.50-19.70 on the 05-14 chart; 18-19 / 0.786 on the 06-02 chart). 18.5 is the midpoint of that fresh demand.

targets.basis: Resistance shelf on both charts (23.50/23.75 on 05-14; ~23.33 on 06-02) — first realistic take-profit.
27.50 HTF resistance / 'take profits, very HTF level' labelled on both charts; 27.21 is the structure-shift trigger just below it.
Newer 2025-06-02 grade target (only reached if 27.21 is overtaken).
Newer 2025-06-02 grade target — aggressive extension, lowest confidence.

safest_plan.stop_basis: Below the 18$ demand floor and the ~17 (0.705) support shown on the 06-02 chart. Older read framed it as 'close below 19 = structural demand breach', but since the buy itself is at 18-19 the stop has to sit beneath the 18 floor; a daily close under ~17 opens the deeper 'still pending' 13-14 zone.

stance_by_source.summary: Still a buy but downgraded to 'weak' — price has slipped to ~20.7 below the prior demand; wants 18-19 on spot or low leverage, targets 30 then 34, invalid on a break of key demand.
'Textbook' buy-the-pullback: 21-22 zone or deeper 19-19.70 demand, take-profits at 23.50 then 27.21/27.50, invalid on a daily close below 19.

conflicts: The older 2025-05-14 'textbook' read said buy the 21-22 zone with invalidation on a close below 19. By 2025-06-02 price had already slipped to ~20.7, breaking that zone, and the same source cut the grade from 8 to 4 and moved the buy down to 18-19 — so the older 21-22 entry is stale and should not be acted on.

How deep the pullback runs is genuinely unsettled: the buyable demand is 18-19, but both charts also flag a much deeper 'still pending' zone around 13-14, so price could keep falling before it's actually buyable.
Chart vs extracted text: the images put the demand at 19.50-19.70 and an 18 level, slightly above the text's flat '18-19' — used the chart zone as the anchor.
plain_english: Both of these reads want to BUY Avalanche on a dip, so they agree on direction. But the more recent one (early June) is much less convinced: the price has already fallen through the 21-22 area that was supposed to be the buy zone, the setup was downgraded from 'textbook' to 'weak', and the buy target was lowered to roughly 18-19. Right now the price (~20.7) is stuck in no-man's-land between that failed zone above and the next solid support below, and there's even a much deeper support around 13-14 that may come into play. Do not buy here — wait for price to reach the 18-19 area and show it holds; if it does, first profit-taking is around 23.5, then 27.5, and treat a daily close below ~17 as the setup being wrong.
